name: pyrotkit

metadata.name: pyrotkit
metadata.summary: A library to help with roguelikes

documentation:
# pyROT

A library to help with the development of roguelikes.

classes.name: pyrotkit.types.coordinate.Coordinate
documentation: A location on a 2D grid

modules.name: pyrotkit.types
documentation: Definitions for various types used throughout the library.

functions.name: pyrotkit.los.bresenham.bresenham
documentation:
Uses Bresenham's algorithm to determin line of sight.

Note that this isn't a field of view algorithm; it **does not
check** that any tile in the line in visible. It would be more
accurate to say it is simply a line algorithm, but in the context
of roguelikes, line of sight seems to be the accepted nomenclature.

Args:
    start: the Tile to start from
    stop: the Tile to look at
Returns:
    A numpy array of tiles in the line

modules.name: pyrotkit.los
documentation: Algorithms for determining line of sight.

functions.name: pyrotkit.los.xiaolinwu.xiaolinwu
documentation:
Caluclates a line using Xiaolin Wu's algorithm.

This produces an anti-aliased line which can be used for
line damage calculations.

Args:
    start: start tile of the line
    stop: end tile of the line
Returns:
    A list of tiles and their "brightness"

Calculates a path using Dijkstra's algorithm.

Args:

classes.name: pyrotkit.types.pathtile.PathTile
documentation:
A tile with distance information for pathfinding.

Has a location on a 2D grid, represented by a {py:class}`Coordinate`.

Args:

: coord: The tile's location
  dist: Distance from the starting tile

functions.name: pyrotkit.tools.funcs.tunnel_between
documentation:
Plots a tunnel between two rooms.

Args:
    start: The starting tile
    stop: The finish tile
    rng: The random generator to use when deciding how to tunnel
Yields:
    tile: tiles in the tunnel

functions.name: pyrotkit.tools.funcs.check_percent_walls
documentation:
Calculates and returns the percent of the map which is walls.

Args:
    map_to_check: Map to check for coverage
    wall: What should be considered a wall
Returns:
    Percent of the map covered in walls

documentation: Get the top-left tile of the camera.

methods.name: pyrotkit.tools.camera.Camera.view_slice
documentation: Get a slice that can be used on an NDArray.

methods.name: pyrotkit.tools.camera.Camera.set_center
documentation:
Set the center of the camera.

Raises:
    AttributeError: If the arguments are the wrong type

Translates world coordinates to facilitate maps larger than the screen.

Recalculates properties when setting the center of the camera's view.

Args:
    lock_view: Whether the camera is kept inside the world

modules.name: pyrotkit.generators
documentation: Utilities to aid in the generation of maps.

classes.name: pyrotkit.generators.cellular.CellularGenerator

methods.name: pyrotkit.generators.cellular.CellularGenerator.process_map
documentation:
Processes a randomized map.

This does four iterations of an algorithm which includes seeding walls on
tiles that have fewer than 3 walls in their neighborhood, followed by 3 iterations
that process the map, but don't seed new tiles.

Args:
    working_map: The map to process

methods.name: pyrotkit.generators.cellular.CellularGenerator.generate_map
documentation:
Generates a new map.

Handles seeding and processing the map automatically.

Returns:
    The new map

Uses an algorithm based on Conway's Game of Life to procedurally generate maps.

Args:
    width: The width of the maps to generate
    height: The height of the maps to generate
    wall: The value inserted into the array when a tile is a wall
    floor: The value inserted into the array when a tile is a floor
    prob: The probability that any given tile will be a wall when randomizing the map

modules.name: pyrotkit.fov
documentation: Classes which implement various Field of View algorithms.

classes.name: pyrotkit.generators.rectroom.RoomGenerator

methods.name: pyrotkit.generators.rectroom.RoomGenerator.tunnel_between
documentation:
Digs a tunnel between two tiles.

Randomly decides whether to travel horizontally or vertically first.

Args:
    start: Starting tile
    stop: Finishing tile
Yields:
    x, y: Coordinates of tiles in the tunnel

methods.name: pyrotkit.generators.rectroom.RoomGenerator.generate_map
documentation:
Generates and returns a map made of rectangular rooms.

Returns:
    The map
    The entrance tile
    The exit tile

Generates maps made of rectangular rooms connected by corridors.

Args:
    max_rooms: The maximum number of rooms in the map
    room_min_size: The minimum size when determining a room's dimensions
    room_max_size: The maximum size when determining a room's dimensions

classes.name: pyrotkit.generators.mapgen.MapGenerator

methods.name: pyrotkit.generators.mapgen.MapGenerator.dimensions
documentation: The dimensions of the map

methods.name: pyrotkit.generators.mapgen.MapGenerator.fill
documentation:
Makes and returns a new map full of the given value.

Args:
    value: The value to fill the new map with.
Returns:
    The new map

methods.name: pyrotkit.generators.mapgen.MapGenerator.generate_map
documentation:
Generate a map using the derived generator.

Subclasses **must** override this function.

Raises:
    NotImplementedError: Someone either misused this class or forgot to override this function.

methods.name: pyrotkit.generators.mapgen.MapGenerator.get_neighbors
documentation:
Returns the number of walls which neighbor a given tile.

By default, this counts neighbors in all 8 tiles which touch the specificed tile. Custom
direcitons can be supplied to support maps of other shapes.

Args:
    tile_map: The map in which the tile lives
    tile: The `Tile` whose neighbors to count
    directions: The relative coordinates of all the tiles to check
Returns:
    The number of neighboring walls

methods.name: pyrotkit.generators.mapgen.MapGenerator.get_neighborhood
documentation:
Returns the number of wall tiles in a given tile's neighborhood.

A neighborhood is defined as the tiles within two movements of the specified tile. For
a rectangular grid map with movement in 8 directions, this is the 5x5 grid centered on
the specified tile.

Args:
    tile_map: The map in which the tile lives
    coordinates: The coordinates of the tile
Returns:
    The number of walls in the neighborhood

Base class for all other map generators to inherit from.

Args:
    width: Width of the map
    height: Height of the map
    wall: What to set for wall tiles
    floor: What to set for floor tiles
    dirs: How many directions of movement entity's have
    rng: Random generator to use when making decisions
    dtype: Arrays will be generated with this dtype set, if provided

classes.name: pyrotkit.fov.precise_permissive.PrecisePermissiveView

methods.name: pyrotkit.fov.precise_permissive.PrecisePermissiveView.get_view
documentation:
Executes the algorithm cenetered on the supplied tile.

Args:
    center: Center of the field
    radius: Distance of vision
    visible_callback: function called for each visible tile

Executes the precise permissive view algorithm from roguebasin.com.

Args:
    map: dimensions of the map
    passes_light: function that returns whether the tile is see through
